title: The Epidemiological Characteristics and Spatiotemporal Clustering of Measles - China, 2005-2022.
ehr_evidence: We utilized data from the China Disease Prevention and Control Information System.
summary: A spatiotemporal analysis of measles epidemiology in China from 2005 to 2022 to identify trends, hotspots, and high-risk populations.
key_findings: 732,218 measles cases were reported between 2005 and 2022.
The incidence rate reached a historical low of 0.039 per 100,000 in 2022.
A high-incidence cluster was identified from 2005 to 2008 in the western, central, and northern regions of China.
limitations: Relies on reported cases from a surveillance system
